other_observers: Joyce Takimine, John Drummond and two others from Boulder, CO.; Ms. Metts, from Castlewood Canyon S.P. on 3 July first spotted the bird in about the same area.
specific_location: Seen from Holmstead Parking lot in Castlewood Canyon State Park soaring above the closest cliff to the west.
description: This was a large soaring bird with broad mostly black wings, with silvery gray wingtips. The wingtips were fingerlike projections contrasting strongly with the othewise blackish bird. The width and coloration of the wings made it apparent the bird was a vuluture and not a raptor.   The blackish tail was short and squared off. The head was small and lighter than the black body, wings, and tail.Behaviors: Soared without flapping for 15 minutes from 10:05 to 10:20 A.M. Used thermals of cliff for lift.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
